programme.title: Mountain Monsters
programme.sub-title: The Secret of the Red Shed
programme.desc: The hunters track down one of the mysterious cloaked figures at the heart of the Spearfinger mystery. However, the red shed might hold all the answers

programme.title: My Cat from Hell

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps a woman whose cat has not used a litter box for two years, and another troubled feline terrorises a fellow cat and its owner

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson returns to Texas to build a sophisticated treehouse in the heart of Dallas. He designs a clean-lined modern structure with a luxurious touch

programme.title: Meet the Superbrains
programme.desc: Two teams of scientists demonstrate why electricity could help end humanitys reliance on fossil fuels, and build an electric speedboat and plane to prove their point

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts answer more questions about the animal kingdom, including whether dogs feel guilty when they do something wrong and why tigers are orange

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Investigating what dogs see when they watch TV, how snakes eat prey twice the size of their heads, and how cuttlefish are able to vanish without a trace

programme.title: Extinct or Alive
programme.desc: In Indonesia, Forrest Galante seeks the Millers grizzled langur, or Dracula monkey. When he hears of an illegal pet market, his search becomes a rescue mission

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Browns bring a haul to Port Alexander, and poor weather becomes the least of their problems when the Integrity becomes stranded in the middle of the water

programme.desc: The pressure is on the team to finish transforming actor Tracy Morgans pool house into an aquarium big enough to house his sharks

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Otto surveys the condition of his rusting barge after a winter in drydock. Elsewhere, Eivin and Eve use explosives to clear land for a new root cellar

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: A broken-down bulldozer sends Jeremy on an airborne cross-country mission, and Jenny finds herself facing off with a fierce predator as she is stalked in the wild by a wolf

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Jeremy must enter an uneasy alliance with an old enemy as he makes plans to expand his farmstead, and Neil tackles some major upgrades as he prepares to sell the town

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: Specialists armed with the latest technology set out to locate the fabled bigfoot. The start of their search takes them to the forests of Oregon

programme.title: Swimming: Phelps v Shark
programme.desc: Documentary in which former Olympic swimmer Michael Phelps tests his skills in the water by competing in a race against a shark - one of the worlds fastest marine predators

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: A game of underwater copycat shows how smart dolphins really are. Plus, how hyenas can eat pretty much anything and geckos ability to apparently defy gravity

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How humpback whales are able to leap so far out of the water, why dogs tilt their heads and how do spiders shed their own skin. Plus, why do animals gather in swarms?

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps a man with Crohns disease who is worried about his cats constant moaning, and also diagnoses the problems of an old-age feline

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy looks at a cat who is coming between two lovers. Meanwhile, Jon and Michelles marriage is riddled with stress over their angry feline Zooey

programme.desc: Pete Nelson and his gang ride out to build a double-decker hideout in Texas Hill Country, featuring a saloon-style first floor

programme.desc: Documentary exploring the huge numbers of olive ridley sea turtles that migrate annually to Costa Rica: a country also home to capuchin, howler monkeys and the Hercules beetle

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: The birds that can fly as high as a passenger jet, and why tiny jumping spiders are dancing for their lives. Plus, how West Coast squirrels have outsmarted rattlesnakes

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?

programme.desc: How homing pigeons are able to find their way home, how spiders lure bugs into their trap, and how some fish can survive for years above ground

programme.desc: Bradley and Billy are in the Mojave Desert, California, to find the bombardier beetle. When attacked or agitated, it releases a hot, noxious chemical spray

programme.title: Little Giants

programme.title: Monster Tag
programme.desc: Top athletes including Olympic champion, Lindsey Vonn, team up with scientists to learn about the oceans top predators, using the latest satellite tagging technology

programme.title: Boomtown Rats: Sight and Sound in Concert
programme.desc: DJ Pete Drummond introduces a classic concert by Bob Geldofs band from the Goldiggers, then a popular nightclub and concert venue in Chippenham, Wiltshire. The programme was first shown in February 1984, the same year that the lead singer would go on to found the charity Band Aid, raising money to combat poverty in Ethiopia

programme.title: The Irish Rock Story: A Tale of Two Cities
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of how rock music evolved in Ireland over 40 years. The film demonstrates how the sounds that emerged were grounded in the musical traditions of the islands two main cities, from the pioneering touring showbands of the late 1950s through to the modern day. Richard Dormer narrates an examination of how the harder sounds of Belfast merged with the more folk-centric traditions of Dublin, celebrates some of the key musicians to have emerged from the Irish scene, and reveals how they were influenced by the political, social and cultural climate of their surroundings. With contributions from U2, Sinead OConnor and Bob Geldof

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 May 1999 and featuring Texas, Sugar Ray, Travis, Shania Twain, Sixpence None the Richer, Hepburn and Shanks and Bigfoot

programme.title: Wildlife on One
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of an abandoned baby grey squirrel found by chance in the Oxfordshire countryside. First broadcast in 1981

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents the silver jubilee edition of the old-time music hall programme from January 1978, with Ken Dodd, Jan Hunt and members of the Players Theatre, London, taking to the stage of the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.sub-title: A Rose by Any Other Name
programme.desc: A middle-aged widows family become convinced a photographer is only marrying her for money and ask Hetty to investigate. Given a week to probe his past and find out whether hes a gold-digging cad or a 24-carat gentleman, the unlikely sleuth unearths some extremely interesting facts. Drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield

programme.title: The Turkish Detective
programme.desc: When Ayse and Suleyman are sent to investigate a house fire, all seems innocuous at first, but they soon turn up a clue that may break new ground in Suleymans search for Kayra Khan. Theyre even more surprised when the person who blocks them investigating it is the chief inspector Ikmen

programme.title: The Turkish Detective
programme.desc: The team finally learns the truth about Kayra Khan, but bringing someone to justice will be tough on everyone, while Chief inspector Ikmen faces an uphill struggle to convince his superiors to commit to his plan. Crime drama, starring Haluk Bilginer and Ethan Kai

programme.title: Parkinson
programme.sub-title: Joanna Lumley, Ian McKellen, Neil Morrissey and Marion Montgomery
programme.desc: Michael Parkinson is joined by actress Joanna Lumley, actors Ian McKellen and Neil Morrissey and American jazz singer Marion Montgomery. First broadcast in 1999

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth feels harassed by a combination of working at a charity shop, trying to impress Councillor Nugent and worrying about Roses love life. To cap it all, Daisy informs her that a strange woman in Daddys bedroom claims to be his fiancee. Starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: The latest woman in Timothys life has a jealous ex-boyfriend who does not take kindly to him trying to steal her away. Ronnie Corbett stars

programme.title: Nature and Us: A History Through Art
programme.desc: James Fox tells the story of the human races ever-changing relationship with nature through the lens of some of the worlds most extraordinary artworks, beginning with a look back at the art of the ancient world, looking at the art of prehistoric hunters, the advent of agriculture, the first cities, and the beginnings of Hinduism and Christianity

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.sub-title: A Rose by Any Other Name
programme.desc: A middle-aged widows family become convinced a photographer is only marrying her for money and ask Hetty to investigate. Given a week to probe his past and find out whether hes a gold-digging cad or a 24-carat gentleman, the unlikely sleuth unearths some extremely interesting facts. Drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents the silver jubilee edition of the old-time music hall programme from January 1978, with Ken Dodd, Jan Hunt and members of the Players Theatre, London, taking to the stage of the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Wildlife on One
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of an abandoned baby grey squirrel found by chance in the Oxfordshire countryside. First broadcast in 1981

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Author Patrick Wright explores the architecture of Dorset, including the Church of St Peter and St Paul in Blandford, Milton Abbey, and the Old Higher Lighthouse at Portland

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: Bill Oddie narrates this look at the behaviour of stoats, as cameras capture the exploits of a female raising her young in the grounds of the 18th-century mansion in Derbyshire

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: Water has always been the driving force in East Anglia - its ebb and flow has shaped the fenland. The ancient wetlands once covered hundreds of square miles, but today, well over 90 per cent has been drained and converted into farmland. Every winter, thousands of whooper swans return to the flooded pastures of the Ouse washes from their Arctic breeding grounds. On the Welney Wetlands, spring sees mad march hares boxing over mates. It was once thought these bouts were male hares boxing for dominance, but it is often the females throwing the punches to fend off the attentions of over-eager suitors. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: The Bridges That Built London with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The historian explores the citys Thames crossings, discovering there is much more to them than simply a means of getting from one place to another. Beginning in the marshes of the estuary, he moves upstream to Vauxhall, where archaeologists have recorded Londons oldest bridge, and also learns about the legend of London Bridge, before reaching journeys end at the Millennium Bridge near St Pauls Cathedral

programme.title: Janet Suzman Remembers - Hedda Gabler
programme.desc: The actress looks back on her acclaimed portrayal of Henrik Ibsens formidable heroine in a 1972 BBC adaptation, directed by Waris Hussein and also starring Ian McKellen as Heddas insufferable husband Tesman, with Jane Asher as childhood friend Thea. Reflecting on Heddas fascinating complexity, emotional depth and human flaws, Suzman describes the task of stepping fully into a characters worldview

programme.title: Ibsen: Hedda Gabler
programme.desc: Michael Meyers 1972 adaptation of Henrik Ibsens play about a newlywed woman who is feeling trapped and dangerous. Janet Suzman and Ian McKellen star

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe reveals why breathing is so important to becoming still and being in the moment, illustrated with natural history footage

programme.title: The Bridges That Built London with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The historian explores the citys Thames crossings, discovering there is much more to them than simply a means of getting from one place to another. Beginning in the marshes of the estuary, he moves upstream to Vauxhall, where archaeologists have recorded Londons oldest bridge, and also learns about the legend of London Bridge, before reaching journeys end at the Millennium Bridge near St Pauls Cathedral

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Author Patrick Wright explores the architecture of Dorset, including the Church of St Peter and St Paul in Blandford, Milton Abbey, and the Old Higher Lighthouse at Portland

The ancient wetlands once covered hundreds of square miles, but today, well over 90 per cent has been drained and converted into farmland. Every winter, thousands of whooper swans return to the flooded pastures of the Ouse washes from their Arctic breeding grounds. On the Welney Wetlands, spring sees mad march hares boxing over mates. It was once thought these bouts were male hares boxing for dominance, but it is often the females throwing the punches to fend off the attentions of over-eager suitors. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Paul Ainsworth, Jess Filbey and Tom Parker Bowles, and special guest Alex Kingston. Olly Smith picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

They were also beaten 22-15 by Italy when the teams met at Stadio Olimpico in last years competition. With analysis from Sam Warburton, Martin Johnson, and Sergio Parisse, and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Jonathan Davies, and John Barclay

programme.sub-title: This Wasnt the Plan. But Its Where We Are

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester United (Kick-off 2.15pm). All the action from the showpiece match, held at Ashton Gate in Bristol. This is the seventh successive season in which Chelsea have reached the final of this competition, and they lifted the trophy last term with a 2-1 win over Manchester City. In contrast, this is the first time United have reached the final of this competition, and should they prevail itll be only their second major honour, with their first coming two seasons ago when they won the FA Cup

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Anita Rani head to Devon for a special lambing season episode. From scanning ewes for pregnancy, to the rush of new births, and bottle-feeding vulnerable lambs through their early days, Adam and Anita look at all stages of lambing, following the teamwork, care and expertise that bring new life safely into the world in a county shaped by generations of sheep farming

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Thirlestane Castle in the Scottish Borders, where treasures include a Rod Stewart Gold Disc a silver chalice thought to have been found in a potato field, and a gold, enamelled snuffbox gifted by a Russian Tsar. Fiona also looks into the history of the famous Paisley shawl, and reveals the personal stories behind three antiques belonging to Silverware Expert Gordon Foster

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Period drama based on Janice Hadlows novel, telling the story of the overlooked middle sister in Pride and Prejudice, both during the events of Jane Austens classic novel and beyond. The Meryton assembly brings Marys first taste of romance, but also leaves her forced to decide between trying to please her family and forging a path of her own. Starring Ella Bruccoleri and Richard E Grant

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: After disappointment at the ball, Mary sets aside hopes of finding a match and devotes herself to more serious pursuits. But Marys resolve is tested when Mr Bennets distant cousin, Mr Collins, visits Longbourn with a surprising proposal. Mary contemplates a decision which could save her family from ruin, but is she really willing to suppress her true feelings to secure her familys future?

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: More video evidence of the attack emerges. Careys assertion that an insidious conspiracy is unfolding at the heart of the establishment gains little traction, so she takes matters into her own hands and seeks an unlikely ally in Gemma Garland. Meanwhile, Noah Pierson finds his first day at work more surprising than he ever expected

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield, and Manchester United v Aston Villa at Old Trafford. Liverpool won 2-1 against a Spurs side that ended the game with nine men in the reverse fixture, but the Londoners will have been determined to avoid another defeat to ease their relegation concerns. Villa beat United by the same score when they last met, with Morgan Rogers scoring a goal in either half to inspire them to victory. Plus, Crystal Palace v Leeds United, and Nottingham Forest v Fulham

programme.title: The Womens Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekends matches, including Chelsea v Manchester United in the League Cup final, and Aston Villa v Manchester City in the Super League

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: From Strictly to the sofa, Claudia kicks off her chat show which promises Hollywood actors, the best of British and comedy stars - all put at their ease by the hosts trademark warmth, charm and humour. Her first guests are Hollywood star and musician Jeff Goldblum, actress Vanessa Williams, comedy icon Jennifer Saunders and funnyman Tom Allen

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Escape Room: Tournament of Champions (2021)
programme.desc: The survivors of a deadly game designed by a sinister corporation are dragged back into an even deadlier trap. And as a handful of former escapees join in, the competitors must work together to outsmart their inhuman tormentors and give their all to solve increasingly sophisticated puzzles. Horror sequel, starring Taylor Russell and Logan Miller

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: As the rise of platforms like OnlyFans gives adult entertainment performers financial independence away from the studio model of old, Louis encounters a new generation using their online platforms to call out alleged predatory behaviour by prominent figures from the business. As a result, an industry that prides itself on pushing sexual boundaries is asking itself difficult questions about consent, and infamous figures like long-time performer Ron Jeremy are being challenged in court

programme.title: Living Every Second: The Kris Hallenga Story
programme.desc: Diagnosed with Stage IV breast cancer at 23, Kris Hallenga made a radical decision about what she wants to do with the rest of her life. Together with her twin sister, Maren, Kris founded the Coppafeel charity, harnessing the power of her own experience to show millions of young people how to check for signs of cancer. In 2013, Kris made a film about her life for the BBC and, over the decade that followed, continued to invite the cameras back. This film reveals how her energy, drive and ambition has left an important legacy

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Weeping Angels take over New York in both the present and the past. Rory finds himself drawn back in time to the 1930s, where River Song is playing private eye, and a harvest of human life is being planned. The Doctor and Amy rush to the rescue, only to discover a terrible future may already be written in stone. Alex Kingston and Mike McShane guest star in Karen Gillan and Arthur Darvills final episode

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctors search for Clara, the mysterious woman who died twice centuries apart, takes him to modern-day London and a run-in with a third incarnation. Something dangerous is lurking in the citys wi-fi signals, uploading peoples minds and imprisoning them - and among those targeted is the woman he has travelled through space and time to find. Jenna-Louise Coleman making her first official appearance alongside Celia Imrie

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike and Alison welcome the very first guests to their new bijoux BandB. While serving the couple breakfast, Alison realises that they are not enjoying their break and, fearing a bad review, utilises the surprising skills of Lady Button and Julian to ensure the pair make the most of their weekend away. Thomas is perplexed yet secretly thrilled by the sudden attention from the Plaguers, who, after admiring Alisons discarded portrait of him are now affording him celebrity status in Button House, hanging on to his every word. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mary walks Julian through her journey of self-discovery and how she finally found her voice. Thomas realises enough is enough - he must go cold turkey on his Alison addiction and needs Pats undivided attention. Old friends of Mike check in to the guesthouse and agree to share the surprising secret of their success. The Captain is thrown by a change to his daily routine, so Kitty steps in to teach him how to live in the moment. Robin helps Lady Button to remember lost friends

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: Agnes needs someone to look after Grandad so she can treat herself to a much-needed holiday - but with private nursing homes being so expensive, it seems her only option is to convince the health visitor he has lost his marbles. Rory and Dino move in for a couple of weeks, and the matriarch is soon spitting feathers when the latter begins taking over the house, and her late husband gets in touch from beyond the grave with some ominous news. Hit comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: It seems that everyone in the Brown family has a date for Valentines Day except Agnes, so she turns to the internet to find herself a man. Dermot tries to come up with a loving gesture to prove to Maria he can still be romantic - but he makes the mistake of taking advice from Buster - while Agnes and Winnie are fascinated by Bettys sex manual and Grandad ends up addicted to Viagra. Hit comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: Man Up (2015)
programme.desc: While traveling across London to attend a party to celebrate her parents 10th wedding anniversary, a thirtysomething single woman is mistaken for a recently divorced strangers blind date, so decides to spend a chaotic evening with him. However, she has to face the consequences when he realises she is not his intended blind date. Romantic comedy, starring Lake Bell and Simon Pegg

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Musical extravaganza recalling the night when Janet met Jonny in Runcorn, told in flashback and accompanied by hits in the style of Christina Aguilera and Dr Dre. Viewers get to see the pairs first kiss as well as a chance to see how far Gaz will go to get Donna

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet tries to dump Jonny, but suffers the indignity of being upstaged by some pasties. Louise gets revenge on David, and Donna plucks up the courage to pursue a new dream. Sitcom, starring Ralf Little, Will Mellor, Natalie Casey, Kathryn Drysdale and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny finds a new girlfriend, Gaz challenges David to a drinking contest, and a book has a profound effect on Louise. Sitcom, starring Ralf Little, Natalie Casey, Kathryn Drysdale and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny ruins his first date with Kate by trying to be too macho. A large spider spoils Louises plans to live with Janet, and Gaz tries to become a pop idol. Sitcom, starring Ralf Little, Natalie Casey, Will Mellor and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Lonely Janet and frisky Gaz grow closer than intended and Louise applies for a new job. Jonny and Kate learn how biscuits can spice things up in the bedroom. Comedy, starring Ralf Little, Kathryn Drysdale and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommys recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommys next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mother out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up. Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Brothers, flatmates and polar opposites Samir and Nikhil find themselves on an adventure when they accidentally steal a package from their maniac neighbour. Short, one-off comedy, starring Karan Gill, Ikky Kabir, Eshaan Akbar, Elina Saleh, Ed Kear and Andrea Valls

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: Zara McDermott discovers the dark side of Thailands backpacker culture. In the first episode, she lands in trouble with the Thai police when she starts to uncover the scale of Thailands illegal sex industry. A visit to the Bangkok Immigration Detention Centre exposes how easy it is to get caught on the wrong side of the law

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Under pressure from the gang to get rid of the informant, Mark searches for Ravi before finding him at the Boxing Den. Lauren worries about her future, as news reaches her that the car lot is up for sale, and Phil avoids a situation

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Priya panics about Ravis safety, and heads to find Vicki to reveal all. Following their respective conversations with Peter and Jack, its clear that Max and Lauren both have very different ideas on how their business partnerships is going to run

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Priya is concerned for Ravis mental wellbeing, but Ravi heads to the gym to avoid her questioning. In the cafe, Ross tries to reassure Vicki of her safety, but their mood is soured when Mark arrives and chastises them both for getting involved. Peter supports Lauren as she prepares for her first day as manager of the car lot, but she is upset to learn that Max intends to run the business

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lauren desperately tries to prove herself by selling a classic car, and when a thug attacks her, Mark rushes to Laurens aid. Vicki asks Phil for help, and Oscar leads a smear campaign on Ian at the request of Elaine. However, Beale soon rumbles their plan

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and her team of experts are back to help more families declutter their homes. They begin with the Kings in Fife - retired architectural technician Gerry, care worker Trish and two of their three grown-up children. Their house contains 30 years of memories, knick-knacks and general clutter - not to mention countless unfinished DIY projects. During the process, the team learn Gerry has recently been diagnosed with early onset dementia, so it is important they hold on to items that will continue to spark memories for him while losing any clutter so it is easy for him to move around

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Comedy about five talented black teenagers at a prestigious boarding school. As the boarders gear up for their final term, a shocking act of vandalism throws everything into chaos. With the school on lockdown, Toby has to find a way to break out and see Abby. Leah is under suspicion for the school destruction, and Femi is distracted by a mystery girl. Jaheim wrestles with a dilemma that will change everything

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The students are teamed up with career mentors, but things quickly go wrong. Leah is fuming with Felix about the sabotage, and Femis dreams are crushed. Omar is in denial, and Toby is distracted by Abbys dangerous games. Violence erupts on the hockey pitch, and the students bright futures feel further away than ever

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: This week, the makeover challenge goes Olympic as the queens drag up a host of Team GB Olympians, including Greg Rutherford, Shanaze Reade, Molly Thompson-Smith, Celia Quansah and Daniel Jervis. Pop superstar Anastacia joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Graham Norton

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: Zara McDermott discovers the dark side of Thailands backpacker culture. In the first episode, she lands in trouble with the Thai police when she starts to uncover the scale of Thailands illegal sex industry. A visit to the Bangkok Immigration Detention Centre exposes how easy it is to get caught on the wrong side of the law

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: Zara McDermott meets men chasing cheap thrills in Asias sex capital, Pattaya, and also talks to a local sex worker, who reveals the harsh reality of working in the illegal sex industry. She then heads to Ko Phangan, an exotic island famed for its spirituality and beauty. For Zara, this is paradise - but Lucio, a Brit who built a burgeoning cannabis business here while the drug was decriminalised, reveals that navigating the local law isnt always easy

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Comedy about five talented black teenagers at a prestigious boarding school. As the boarders gear up for their final term, a shocking act of vandalism throws everything into chaos. With the school on lockdown, Toby has to find a way to break out and see Abby. Leah is under suspicion for the school destruction, and Femi is distracted by a mystery girl. Jaheim wrestles with a dilemma that will change everything

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The students are teamed up with career mentors, but things quickly go wrong. Leah is fuming with Felix about the sabotage, and Femis dreams are crushed. Omar is in denial, and Toby is distracted by Abbys dangerous games. Violence erupts on the hockey pitch, and the students bright futures feel further away than ever

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: At War: Trump, Israel and Iran - Panorama
programme.desc: Jane Corbin investigates the origins and aims of the US/Israeli offensive against Iran, and the future of the relationship between the UK and the US in light of Keir Starmers refusal to allow the use of UK bases for the first attacks. The programme asks what the campaign is achieving, how far it could escalate, and what it means for ordinary people caught in the fighting

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from central England must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang are joined by acclaimed film producer, Bafta winner and Oscar nominee Alison Owen. Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent central England at the national finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Veronica must decide her future now she is not currently a nun, but not fully part of the world either. The Maternity Home prepares to close its doors, but Dr Turner is determined to preserve equipment and resources for his patients until the last possible moment. The Mullucks fight for the rights of all Thalidomide victims, while Cyril and Rosalind prepare for their wedding

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piris fall under the spell of a magical merguin, a mermaid-penguin, who has the ability to change their personalities

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Cosima, Big Lizard and Ammon are squabbling over Dads precious explorer notebook when disaster strikes. Only by working together can they save it from being lost for ever

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy is obsessed with playing on his phone and the ducklings decide to confiscate it

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes find treasure as they attempt to save Merhaven from an angry seaweed, and Blossom is delighted with her bath bomb treasure

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings find an ultra-modern glowing cabin they can use for their party

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings try to destroy the statues of Grizzy, but he will not let them ruin his art.

programme.title: Game on Grandparents
programme.desc: Pro-gamer Yammy guides the teams through Fall Guys

programme.title: Bros in Control
programme.desc: The brothers become superheroes, while West End superstar Hannah Lowther joins Joe for a sock puppet musical

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Nitro, Fire and Hammer prank a dad who thinks his son is meeting the Gladiators. However, when Hammer doesn?t appear, he is asked to put on a fake wig, beard and costume and stand in!

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Packed full of amazing celeb guests, epic challenges and lols galore!

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don kicks off the horticultural year by cutting back ornamental grasses, planting summer-fruiting raspberries and potting up dahlias. Adam Frost has moved house, and reveals his new garden. And Arit Anderson gets access behind the scenes at the Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, where they do vital work in conserving endangered plants from all over the world

programme.title: Goodbye, Mr Chips (1969)
programme.desc: A shy, retiring schoolmaster sets out to woo a singing showgirl. He doesnt approve of her profession, but she finds life as an on-campus housewife hard to adjust to. Romantic musical based on James Hiltons novel, starring Peter OToole, Petula Clark, Michael Redgrave, George Baker and Sian Phillips

programme.title: Sweet Charity (1969)
programme.desc: A lonely but optimistic dancer in a seedy nightclub goes in search of true love, but finds instead a succession of deceitful men who only let her down. When she meets a shy, claustrophobic man in a lift, she thinks he might be the one - but tries to keep her past a secret. Musical, with Shirley MacLaine, John McMartin, Ricardo Montalban and Sammy Davis Jr, and featuring songs including The Rhythm of Life and Big Spender

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs representing central England serve their canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry, with Michelin-starred chef Spencer Metzger as this weeks veteran judge. Highlights include a Jerusalem artichoke and turnip dish linked to War Horse and a monochrome, art-deco styled monkfish dish that pays homage to Birminghams Electric Theatre. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining chefs representing central England serve up their mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. They include a duck breast and celeriac bread sauce celebrating the special effects team who worked on Inception, as well as an edible film reel with white chocolate and ginger mousse. Veteran judge Spencer Metzger helps to decide who deserves to stay and who should go. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the results and this afternoons news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Host Vernon Kay and unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton test Owain Wyn Evans, Greg Rutherford and Abby Cook on their knowledge of the UK, beginning with Belfast

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Will Axon and Thomas Plant asses items in a visit Southend-on-Sea, with presenter Paul Martin meeting the son of a man who launched trampolining in Essex in the 1950s

programme.title: Jane Reas Bloomin Wild
programme.desc: It is winter in Shilnavogie, and the daily routine of feeding animals and keeping the farm tidy is easier said than done now that Jane is seven months pregnant. In the polytunnel, Jane is already thinking ahead to next year, where her visit to Glenarm Castle Walled Garden in the spring has inspired her to start a tulip patch of her own. She is also on a mission to save her dwindling flock of Jacob sheep

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Neglected Birds Found and Highland Blaze Seen from Space
programme.desc: An emergency in Hertfordshire uncovers a case of animal neglect and in the Scottish Highlands, fire crews battle a wildfire so large its visible from space. Paul Colgan takes part in first aid training catered specifically to farmers in Dumfries

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Music veterans and old friends Francis Rossi and Linda Nolan join experts Philip Serrell and Natasha Raskin Sharp on an antiques pleasure cruise around Surrey and Sussex. Its fitting they have an iconic 1970s motor to nip around in and £400 each to spend on some antique delights they both hope will make big profits at the Worcestershire auction

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya begins in Germanys Bavarian mountains, paragliding over Neuschwanstein Castle before travelling to meet the mayor of Kallstadt, the ancestral home of the Trump family, and heading to Ramstein, Americas largest airbase in Europe Ramstein. Crossing to Spain, Katya begins in the region of Catalonia, discovering how its strong nationalist identity ties into the legacy of dictator Francisco Franco

programme.title: Liza: A Truly Terrific Absolutely True Story (2024)
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on actress and singer Liza Minnellis life during the 1970s, just after the death of her mother, when she confronted a range of personal and professional challenges. It was a period that saw her seek out a number of extraordinary mentors - Kay Thompson, Fred Ebb, Charles Aznavour, Halston and Bob Fosse. They all helped her to transform from a performer with boundless raw talent and famous parents to a mature, polished superstar of stage and screen

programme.title: Cabaret (1972)
programme.desc: A flighty nightclub singer in 1930s Berlin is drawn into a love triangle with a sensitive English writer and an aristocratic German, as the Nazi party grows in might and the country heads inexorably toward war. Oscar-winning musical directed and choreographed by Bob Fosse, starring Liza Minnelli, Joel Grey, Michael York and Helmut Griem

programme.title: New York, New York (1977)
programme.desc: An egotistical saxophonist starts a turbulent love affair with an up-and-coming vocalist at the end of World War Two as they both struggle to make it in the Big Band era. Martin Scorseses musical drama, starring Robert De Niro, Liza Minnelli, Lionel Stander, Barry Primus and Georgie Auld

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The reetective drama, starring Holliday Grainger. Whistleblower Rachel Carey is ready to launch a revolutionary new camera network capable of catching live deep-fakes in action. With the home secretary Isaac Turners support, the changes signal a new dawn in UK counter-terrorism policing, at least until a terror attack in central London sends shockwaves through the nation, leaving Rachel the one key eyewitness

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: Life coach Teresa hosts a wild walk in Kielder Forest. It remains to be seen whether her traditional Northumbrian picnic will find her in pole position by the end

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Vick Hope explore sugar beet harvesting in the east of England, 100 years after sugar was first grown in this country. In Norfolk, Vick meets farmers racing to lift the last of this years crop, while Charlotte visits one of the countrys biggest processing plants. Plus, Tom Heap investigates whats really in the sewage sludge spread as fertiliser

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: Ian Hamilton and his guide dog Major head to the West Lothian town of Bathgate, which has an incredible industrial past and now a popular commuter area for people working in Edinburgh. Ian helps out in an award-winning butchers and offers to do some taste testing, then works it off by climbing a hill to find some neolithic remains. He investigates the towns historic theatre and meets a local brass band before getting the chance to relax in Scotlands biggest floatation centre

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from central England must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang are joined by acclaimed film producer, Bafta winner and Oscar nominee Alison Owen. Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent central England at the national finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Lover Come Back (1961)
programme.desc: Advertising executive Carol Templeton is determined to outdo her competitor Jerry Webster at every opportunity. When an important campaign comes up, she tries to beat him to it and clinch the deal, whatever the price. Unfortunately for her, she fails to realise the whole thing is a set-up devised by her hated rival. Romantic comedy, starring Doris Day, Rock Hudson, Tony Randall, Edie Adams and Jack Oakie

programme.title: Pillow Talk (1959)
programme.desc: A man and woman who have never met squabble about each others use of a shared telephone line. One day their paths cross by chance and he is instantly smitten with her. Quickly realising who she really is, he poses as a perfect gentleman from the South to woo her. Romantic comedy, starring Rock Hudson, Doris Day, Tony Randall, Thelma Ritter, Nick Adams and Julia Meade

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team visit Hopetoun House not far from Edinburgh. Antiques experts James Lewis and Adam Partridge are on hand to value items, and Paul discovers the architectural history of the venue and why it is such a unique treasure in the Scottish countryside

programme.title: Live Irish League Football
programme.sub-title: Glentoran v Linfield - League Cup Final
programme.desc: Glentoran v Linfield (Kick-off 3.00pm). Stephen Watson presents coverage of the BetMcLean Cup Final from Clearer Twist National Stadium at Windsor Park. The Glens will hope to go one better than last year when they were beaten finalists in this competition, losing 1-0 to Cliftonville after extra time, but to do so theyll have to beat the team that has lifted this trophy more than any other, while The Blues will hope to add to their 12 previous titles, the most recent of which came two seasons ago. With commentary by Thomas Kane

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Gladiator Apollo, on their knowledge of the UK, beginning with Derry/Londonderry

programme.title: The Chronicles of Armagh
programme.desc: Ranger Donald witnesses the start of the deer breeding season and Jane gathers the autumn harvest from the orchard. At the Crooked Lake, Rosie takes satisfaction from a 2.5km open water swim

programme.title: Six Nations Rugby Special
programme.desc: Ugo Monye is joined by Sam Warburton and John Barclay to present action from the final round of fixtures, featuring France v England at Stade de France, Wales v Italy at Principality Stadium and Ireland v Scotland at Aviva Stadium. France were looking to wrap-up another strong campaign in style, while Wales were bidding for a first home win in the competition since 2022, and Scotland were hoping to end an 11-match losing streak against the Irish

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Arran, Callum has to gather their flock from 4,000 acres of steep, rough hill terrain, while Zara recalls the devastating effects of his quadbike accident on that same ground. In Dornoch, Rob and Emma need the vets help when a ewe gets into trouble birthing triplets, while Ally and daughter Bethany travel by boat to gather sheep on an uninhabited island to bring them home to their croft on Lewis

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Abandoned by their dominant males, the Xudum pride comes under siege from more intruder lions, and the mums with young cubs must link up with the rest of the pride for protection. Leopard mum Lediba struggles after a lion steals her kill. To feed her cub, she is forced to make a dangerous move. Pobe is teaching cheetah cubs Kea and Bo to hunt, but when a hyena pack arrives, their survival instincts are pushed to the limit. With predators all around, every mother must fight harder than ever to keep her young alive

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: In the early hours a frantic teenage girl calls 999 to say that her friend has been attacked and stabbed in a car park. Police rush to the scene, where they find a 16-year-old with a life-threatening wound to his stomach. While his life hangs in the balance, West Midlands Polices homicide team get to work, with forensic investigators discovering two discarded weapons at the scene - a kitchen knife and a machete

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.sub-title: Niamh Regan and Caoimhín Ó Raghallaigh
programme.desc: A collaboration in which distinctive Galway singer-songwriter Niamh Regan creates new music with Dublin fiddler and composer Caoimhín Ó Raghallaigh. In Irish

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.desc: Galway multi-instrumentalist, composer and singer-songwriter Naomi Berrill collaborates with west Cork singer-songwriter Molly OMahony. In Irish

programme.title: The Chronicles of Strangford

programme.title: Zero (2022)

When Zeros life is threatened, she flees with Wake, while Serger is forced to chase them down. Faye Gilberts thriller, starring Lauren Grace

programme.title: Small Prophets

Gordon threatens to fire Michael, while Brian has received a letter from an old friend, and a hole in the roof leads to a discovery. Kacey and Michael exhume the prophets from their pile of manure to see if they have reached the state of divination and the ability to predict the future. Comedy written and directed by Mackenzie Crook, starring Pearce Quigley and Lauren Patel

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the game show that asks contestants to complete a series of general knowledge questions faster than The Chaser, a brainbox of the highest order.

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Ricki, Will, Jane and Victoria answer general knowledge questions and work as a team to take on quiz genius the Chaser and secure a cash prize. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and antiques experts Ian Towning, Mike Melody and Tim Hogarth visit Todmorden, West Yorkshire, to offer locals the chance to put items up for auction or accept a cash sum. Among the days highlights is Tims deal on a pewter mirror

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and antiques dealers Ian Towning, Brenda Haller and David Hakeney visit Ulverston near the Lake District, where a rocking horse is sold at auction to raise money for a local hospice

programme.title: Blockbusters
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the general knowledge quiz, in which contestants try to complete a path across or down a board of lettered hexagons

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a charity edition of the game show, with three famous faces all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five dentistry students answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Comedy quiz show with celebrity guests. Presented by Lily Savage

programme.title: Greg Davies: The Back of My Mums Head
programme.desc: Stand-up comedy with the Man Down star, recorded at the Lowry in Salford. Stung by his mothers suggestion that he is not normal, Greg puts everyone else under the microscope in a petty quest to discover if anyone actually is. The tour received rave reviews and was extended to 94 dates, including multiple sell-out nights at Londons Hammersmith Apollo and Southbank Centre

programme.title: Bill Baileys Remarkable Guide to the Orchestra
programme.desc: The comedian joins forces with Oscar-winning composer Anne Dudley to investigate some of the finer nuances of orchestral performance. He teams up with the BBC Concert Orchestra to reveal the lesser-known influences of some of the worlds most celebrated composers, and exhibits some of his own original works

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Bridgeport, Connecticut, to visit Cafe Tavolini, which is being neglected by husband and wife managers teetering on the edge of divorce. The estranged couples two children are struggling to save the business from their parents disputes, and the visiting chef tries to shock the pair into stopping the restaurant from becoming a casualty of their quarrel

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of BandB owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Dunsandles Guest House in New Brighton, Merseyside, where owner Alan Johnson and his professional sidekick Dave Owen hope to win the title - but a scandal with some unwanted hair on the bed sheets is not a good start, and two guests feel they are making an exhibition of themselves with a tea-trolley in full view of the neighbours

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the second day of the competition, the contestants visit the Lady Teal Hotel Boat on the waterways near Burnley in Lancashire, and owners Nick and Gina Mead hope to impress them with an afternoon of entertainment out on the locks. At breakfast, one of the guests admits to not enjoying his stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From York House in Tetbury, Gloucestershire, where Brock Bergius hopes that his cheeky sense of humour and perfectionist nature will help him win the competition. He is in his element making saucy jokes during an afternoon of sausage making, but his reluctance to stray from the menu at breakfast leaves some of his guests disappointed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Heyford House in Oxfordshire, where Leo and Sonya Brooke-Little hope to impress the contestants with their hospitality. The afternoon event is boxing, which Brock Burgius sees as the perfect opportunity to settle some scores with his fellow competitors

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks winner is announced as the BandB owners meet for the last time to thrash out their differences. Feedback includes criticism of a badly positioned tea tray, controversy over a hot chocolate and accusations of overenthusiastic hosting

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: With Capel Salem slowly becoming a home, the next room for transformation is the Victorian-inspired parlour bathroom. Keith and Marj take inspiration from Coventry Cathedral, with Keith making a giant clay candlestick holder, while Marj sets about making her dream lampshade for the bathrooms standard lamp

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Market trader Russell and retired nurse Nicola kick-off proceedings in Essex and the evening seems to go well with one diner polishing off his own food - and everyone elses. The second night sees proud parents Sophie and Elliot host, but their guests find crab claws in their starter. On the third evening romantic duo Gemma and Glen decide to go for a more refined dining experience in the hope of winning the cash prize of £1,000. However, its Gemmas love for burlesque that grabs most attention

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie meet their neighbours after their backyards are destroyed by a hurricane. Carrie disapproves of their idea of combining the gardens when they rebuild - until the couple next door build a pool. Comedy, starring Kevin James and Leah Remini

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Cousin Danny loses his job, so Doug comes under pressure from Uncle Stu to find him a new one at IPS - a situation he is keen to avoid. Meanwhile, Arthur takes part in a test audience for a new film. Comedy, starring Kevin James, Gary Valentine and Jerry Stiller

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Incredible Lightness of Being a Baby
programme.desc: Cletus becomes a helium tycoon. Meanwhile, Marge reunites with baby Hudson

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart accidentally sees a new superhero film before its release, and realises he can use his plot-spoiling powers for his own ends. However, two movie executives will stop at nothing to keep his knowledge secret

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events of day eight of the Games from Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. 2018 gold medallist Menna Fitzpatrick is expected to be among the leading contenders for this years title in the womens vision impaired alpine skiing slalom at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, while 18-year-old Hester Poole is expected to make her debut at the Games. Elsewhere, the mens and womens snowboard banked slalom gets under way at Cortina Para Snowboard Park, with 2022 bronze medal winner Ollie Hill expected to be in action

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Further coverage of day eight of the Games from Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. The second run of the mens and womens snowboard banked slalom takes place at Cortina Para Snowboard Park, where the likes of James Barnes-Miller, Ollie Hall, Matt Hamilton, Dave Zyw, and Nina Sparks will represent ParalympicsGB. The second run of the womens vision impaired alpine skiing slalom also takes place at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, as do the para cross-country skiing finals at Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: The concluding live coverage of day eight of the Games from Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. The mens and womens snowboard banked slalom finals, and womens vision impaired alpine skiing slalom final, are scheduled to come to their conclusions, before attention turns toward the Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium for the gold medal match in the mixed team wheelchair curling competition. China were victorious four years ago at the Games in Beijing following their 8-3 victory over Sweden, while Canada claimed the bronze medal after beating Slovakia by the same score

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.sub-title: 2026 Chinese Grand Prix Sprint Race And Qualifying
programme.desc: Lee McKenzie is joined by David Coulthard, Damon Hill, and Ariana Bravo to present action from the opening sprint race of the campaign and the battle for grid positions for the second round of the season at Shanghai International Circuit. Ferraris Lewis Hamilton won the sprint race here last year with Oscar Piastri of McLaren in second. Piastri then went one better in qualifying by claiming pole position on the starting grid, with Mercedes George Russell joining him on the front row

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Dabi and Devi cant wait to wave goodbye to the grey skies of Croydon and say hola to the sunshine and heat in the seaside town of Gandia in Valencia. Theyre looking for a second home where they can spend up to six months at a time. With a budget of £180,000, Lee Juggurnauth lines up five impressive options, but will any wow Dabi and Devi enough to make the move?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with househunters who were keen to fly the nest. In 2017, Chloe was looking to move out of her parental home and find her own slice of Reading. But Chloes dad, who was helping with funds, had his own idea of what kind of place his daughter should buy, which didnt align with her own thoughts. In 2019, Ella and Grant were looking for their own place to start their lives together. But as Phil soon realised, Grant seemed nervous about moving out of his mums house

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.sub-title: Camping Pod and a Straw Bale House
programme.desc: A man embarks on an incredible adventure as he converts a freefall lifeboat into a yellow submarine camping pod in honour of his late father, while three daughters and their mother create a straw bale house oozing with rustic charm. George continues his world tour of the UK as he experiences an oriental delight on the Isle of Wight. Together with master craftsman Will Hardie, the host makes an ingenious hidden rock pool for their magical secret garden home

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Highlights
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan presents action from day eight of the Games, held in Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy, where ParalympicsGB were expected to compete for medals in the mens and womens snowboard banked slalom finals, and the womens vision impaired alpine skiing slalom final. Other events taking centre stage included the para cross-country skiing finals, the gold medal match in the mixed team wheelchair curling, and the para ice hockey competition, which featured two classification finals

programme.title: The Race for Ancient Egypt in Colour
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary using colourised archive footage to explore archaeology in Egypt between the world wars, as the nation strove for sovereignty. The first episode begins in 1922, when Howard Carters discovery of the tomb of Tutankhamun transformed Egyptology into a global sensation and European and American institutions looked to unearth, and control, Egypts ancient heritage. A tense stand-off between Carter and Egypts new government begins, as the authorities seeks a greater say in how their nations ancient past is managed

programme.title: The Roman Empire by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: Alice arrives in Nimes after crossing the Alps by train. The image of a crocodile is everywhere, commemorating Emperor Augustuss victory over Mark Antony and Cleopatra at the Battle of Actium in 31BCE. In Narbonne, Alice revels in more extraordinary Roman artefacts. The train follows the same route as Romes Via Domitia, eventually leading Alice all the way to Spain

programme.title: Bill Baileys Vietnam
programme.desc: Bill heads to the mountainous region of Da Lat, a French-built hill station surrounded by pine forests, where he tries meditation with monks at the Truc Lam monastery, picks leaves at the Cau Dat tea plantation, and zooms through the verdant hills on a mountain-top zipline. With his head in the clouds and romance in the air, Bill oscillates between kitschy tourism and earnest moments with locals

programme.title: Stillwater (2021)
programme.desc: Unemployed Bill Baker travels from his home in Stillwater, Oklahoma to Marseille, France to visit his estranged daughter Allison, who was imprisoned five years earlier for a murder she claims she didnt commit. Confronted with an insurmountable language barrier, cross-cultural differences and maddening legal complexity, Baker decides to take matters into his own hands to exonerate his only child after failing to get Allisons case re-opened. Crime thriller, starring Matt Damon and Abigail Breslin

programme.title: Mothering Sunday (2021)
programme.desc: A maid living in post-First World War England secretly plans to meet with the man she loves while her employers are away, seizing a last chance to be together before he leaves to marry another woman. Romantic drama, starring Odessa Young, Josh OConnor, Colin Firth and Olivia Colman

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Theres fireworks ahead as some of the cuffed pairs grapple with being chained to their total opposite. Horse trainer and countryside campaigner Claire, whos 48, is a fierce advocate of hard work. Her handcuffed partner, 29-year-old city-loving model and content creator Bambi loves the easy life. Can Bambi keep up with Claires exhausting work schedule? In London, Green Party councillor Frank, whos 27, and housing lawyer Lin, whos 38, seem to be getting along famously. But Franks in for a shock that leaves him speechless

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Some of the pairs have had enough and called it quits. But not 70-year-old Yorkshireman Bob and 38-year-old London youth worker Chris. However, when the latter learns about a shocking incident from his partners past, he wonder whether he can continue. Meanwhile, eco-warrior and vegan campaigner Morag is shocked when the woman she is chained to, petrol-head and beauty salon owner Angie, reveals her views on climate change. Hosted by Jonathan Ross

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Single mother Jo Beck wants to move her family to Derby, despite her children being happily settled in Nottingham. Phil Spencer helps her decide if relocating is the right choice and searches for a suitable home. Also in Derby, Kirstie Allsopp offers advice to outdoor sports enthusiasts Liz and John Fairfield, who are looking for a property with a large garden, storage space for their sporting equipment and no noise from nearby roads

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes a union representative at the power plant and immediately uses his new-found authority to save the companys free dental plan and avoid having to pay for Lisas new braces. Featuring the voice of Dr Joyce Brothers

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: So Its Come to This: A Simpsons Clip Show
programme.desc: Barts latest prank wrecks the house and lands Homer in hospital, where he and the family reminisce about all the other injuries and mishaps he has suffered in his eventful and disaster-filled life

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Part one of two. Carrie discovers she is expecting a baby. Deacon and Kelly make a move to reconcile, leaving Spence out on a limb, and Arthur asks Doug to build him a bathroom in the basement. Comedy, starring Kevin James and Leah Remini

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Part two of two. Doug takes on a second job as a chauffeur to supplement his income, pressured by the expense of the new baby and bathroom. Meanwhile, Deacon and Kelly are reconciled with each other - leaving Spence all alone. Comedy, starring Kevin James

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge puts a screen-time limit on the rest of the family, but soon realises that she might be the one who is addicted

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart starts to bond with Ned, prompting a jealous Homer to decide to start mentoring Nelson in retaliation

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the ninth and final day of the Games from Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. The first run of the mens vision impaired alpine skiing slalom final takes place at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, where ParalympicsGB stars Neil Simpson, Sam Cozens and Fred Warburton are expected to be in action. Attention will then turn to the Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium where a number of para cross-country skiing finals take place, including the mens 20km interval start sitting event, where Scott Meenagh is likely to bring his career to an end. Meenagh was a Scotland U18 international rugby player before losing his legs after stepping on an IED in Afghanistan at the age of 21, and went on to become the first Briton to win a medal in para biathlon, claiming silver in the 12.5km individual event at the 2023 World Para Nordic Skiing Championships. With reports from Alice Tai and Dame Sarah Storey

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Concluding coverage of the ninth and final day of the Games from Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. Medals will be decided in the second run of the mens vision impaired alpine skiing slalom final at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, with British stars Neil Simpson, Sam Cozens, and Fred Warburton all hoping for a place on the podium. Elsewhere, the para ice hockey competition is brought to a conclusion with the bronze- and gold-medal matches at Milano Santagiulia Ice Hockey Arena, the latter of which is the final action of the Games. With reports from Alice Tai and Dame Sarah Storey

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Lee McKenzie is joined by David Coulthard, Damon Hill, and Ariana Bravo to present action from the second round of the season at Shanghai International Circuit, where McLaren drivers Oscar Piastri and Lando Norris finished first and second last season. While the race itself proved to be relatively uneventful, the aftermath was anything but, with Charles Leclerc of Ferrari and Pierre Gasly of Alpine being disqualified due to their cars being underweight, Leclercs teammate Lewis Hamilton also being disqualified for excessive skid wear, and In the days that followed, Red Bull announcing that Liam Lawson would undergo a driver swap with Racing Bulls Yuki Tsunoda

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first destination of the week is the Horseshoe Hotel in Egton Bridge, North Yorkshire, where a fancy machine causes confusion but breakfast and eco toiletries prove a big hit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB battle continues in Market Bosworth at the Dixie Arms, run by Alex, whose blingy beds impress two of the guests. However, not all of the facilities are quite as successful

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Bradburys of Blackpool, where former flight attendant Matt is hoping to change his guests perceptions of the seaside town

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to Little Oakhurst, in Stonyhurst near Clitheroe, run by husband-and-wife team Andy and Joanne. The guests are immediately captivated by the stunning location, with views over the Lancashire countryside, though the optional and additional breakfast brings some mixed reviews. At feedback time, it becomes clear that glamping isnt for everyone

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment Day sees Craig and Greta keen to get to the bottom of facilities faults, before Alex points the finger at them about some unsavoury feedback scores. Matt wants to question Alexs cleanliness comments, and Joanne and Andy have some beef over their breakfast scores

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: In Dartmouth, Nigel Cooper sails his steamboat Halcyon along the River Dart for the clubs annual competition. His boat has won twice in the past - can he make it a hat-trick? In Cornwall, farrier and horseman David Jones is hoping his age-old skills will be passed down the line to daughter Myrtle, as they head out to tackle a rampant bracken problem in an old woodland. And in Cornwalls Trebah Gardens, head gardener Darren Dickey embarks on a new project. To celebrate 30 years of the garden becoming a public trust, hes creating a new Victorian-style stumpery with an array of ferns to delight visitors

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan, Billy Monger, Ed Jackson, Sarah Storey, Sean Rose and Alice Tai present live coverage of the event from Cortina dAmpezzo, as the Paralympic flame is extinguished, signaling the end of the 14th Winter Games. Athletes, artists and spectators from all over the world gather in the Cortina Olympic Curling Stadium to enjoy a spectacular final show. The presenting team look back at all the standout stories and key moments from nine days of elite competition spanning six sports

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on The Capture, Married at First Sight Australia, Louis Theroux: Inside the Manosphere, The Floor, Will and Ralf Should Know Better, Britains Best Mattress: How to Sleep Better and This Morning (AI boyfriend)

programme.title: The Son (2022)
programme.desc: New York lawyer Peter Miller receives a panicked visit from his ex-wife Kate. Their 17-year-old son Nicholas has been playing truant from school for a month. Peter allows Nicholas to move in with him but the teenager continues to suffer, unable to articulate the darkness that consumes him. Drama, starring Hugh Jackman, Vanessa Kirby and Anthony Hopkins

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Los Angeles to help La Frite, an outdated French restaurant that is failing to make any profits. His first step is to try to reconcile the feuding family members who run the business, and so devises a plan to bring them together by using secret filming

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa tries to persuade her family not to take part in the towns annual snake-clubbing ritual, but her words fall on deaf ears as Homer anticipates the event with glee. With the guest voice of Barry White

programme.title: Bill Baileys Remarkable Guide to the Orchestra
